message.content:
This section defines and characterizes "二次元" (ACG/2D) mobile games across six interconnected dimensions, supporting retrieval queries on cultural boundaries, design frameworks, visual evolution, gameplay mechanics, monetization strategies, and audience segmentation.

- **Topics & Entities:** Covers the conceptual relationship between "二次元" and "ACGN"; Japanese anime, manga, games, and light novels; character development assets (illustrations, 3D models, voice acting, narrative, combat systems); visual aesthetics (Japanese anime, Korean manhwa, Chinese comics); mainstream gameplay genres (card battles, ARPG, dating simulation/nurturing); PVP and PVE modes; gacha/扭蛋 mechanics; and player demographics (core vs. casual/broad ACG players).
- **Definitions:** Clarifies "二次元" as a cultural attribute rather than a fixed genre; defines "核心塑造" (Core Character Shaping) as a character-centric design strategy aimed at fostering emotional bonds; outlines the expanded boundaries of the "二次元" visual style; and provides a report-specific classification for ACG mobile games based on primary targeting of core ACG players.
- **Claims:** States that Japanese cultural products drive the 二次元-ACGN association, though iterative updates can shift initially excluded games into the category via player community recognition. Asserts that visual aesthetics have evolved from early Japanese dominance to inclusively recognize Korean and Chinese comic styles. Notes that gameplay mechanics are diverse and not restricted to PVP or PVE. Identifies gacha as the dominant monetization model, explaining its design logic through the behavioral psychology framework of the "Skinner box." Distinguishes core players by their cultural affinity and investment willingness, while clarifying that these traits alone do not automatically classify a product.
- **Dates:** References 2025 as the report's contextual year.
- **Procedures, Tables, Figures, Metrics, Evidence Types:** No procedural steps, quantitative metrics, tables, or figures are present. Evidence types are limited to qualitative cultural claims, conceptual definitions, and theoretical psychological analogies (Skinner box) used to explain monetization mechanics.

This section defines and characterizes "二次元" (ACG/2D) mobile games across six interconnected dimensions, supporting retrieval queries on cultural boundaries, design frameworks, visual evolution, gameplay mechanics, monetization strategies, and audience segmentation.

- **Topics & Entities:** Covers the conceptual relationship between "二次元" and "ACGN"; Japanese anime, manga, games, and light novels; character development assets (illustrations, 3D models, voice acting, narrative, combat systems); visual aesthetics (Japanese anime, Korean manhwa, Chinese comics); mainstream gameplay genres (card battles, ARPG, dating simulation/nurturing); PVP and PVE modes; gacha/扭蛋 mechanics; and player demographics (core vs. casual/broad ACG players).
- **Definitions:** Clarifies "二次元" as a cultural attribute rather than a fixed genre; defines "核心塑造" (Core Character Shaping) as a character-centric design strategy aimed at fostering emotional bonds; outlines the expanded boundaries of the "二次元" visual style; and provides a report-specific classification for ACG mobile games based on primary targeting of core ACG players.
- **Claims:** States that Japanese cultural products drive the 二次元-ACGN association, though iterative updates can shift initially excluded games into the category via player community recognition. Asserts that visual aesthetics have evolved from early Japanese dominance to inclusively recognize Korean and Chinese comic styles. Notes that gameplay mechanics are diverse and not restricted to PVP or PVE. Identifies gacha as the dominant monetization model, explaining its design logic through the behavioral psychology framework of the "Skinner box." Distinguishes core players by their cultural affinity and investment willingness, while clarifying that these traits alone do not automatically classify a product.
- **Dates:** References 2025 as the report's contextual year.
- **Procedures, Tables, Figures, Metrics, Evidence Types:** No procedural steps, quantitative metrics, tables, or figures are present. Evidence types are limited to qualitative cultural claims, conceptual definitions, and theoretical psychological analogies (Skinner box) used to explain monetization mechanics.
